methods.name: Pi
methods.description: Return the IEEE-754 single-precision value of π (3.14159265…) in DR0.
comments: load self-data-R cap (c-list[0]) into CR0 — grants read-only access to embedded data words
read data word [0] (π = 3.14159265…) via CR0 into DR0
return π in DR0 to caller

methods.name: E
methods.description: Return the IEEE-754 single-precision value of e (2.71828182…) in DR0.
comments: load self-data-R cap (c-list[0]) into CR0 — grants read-only access to embedded data words
read data word [1] (e = 2.71828182…) via CR0 into DR0
return e in DR0 to caller

methods.name: Phi
methods.description: Return the IEEE-754 single-precision golden ratio φ (1.61803398…) in DR0.
comments: load self-data-R cap (c-list[0]) into CR0 — grants read-only access to embedded data words
read data word [2] (φ = 1.61803398…) via CR0 into DR0
return φ in DR0 to caller

methods.name: Zero
methods.description: Return IEEE-754 positive zero (0.0) in DR0.
comments: load self-data-R cap (c-list[0]) into CR0 — grants read-only access to embedded data words
read data word [3] (0.0) via CR0 into DR0
return 0.0 in DR0 to caller

methods.name: One
methods.description: Return IEEE-754 1.0 in DR0.
comments: load self-data-R cap (c-list[0]) into CR0 — grants read-only access to embedded data words
read data word [4] (1.0) via CR0 into DR0
return 1.0 in DR0 to caller

methods.name: Add
methods.description: Store a user-supplied 32-bit value XYZ in the next free pool slot and return a sealed, read-only Abstract GT capability pointing to that slot. Returns FAULT if the pool (14 slots) is full.
inputs: DR0: XYZ — any IEEE-754 or raw 32-bit value to store
outputs: CR0: Abstract GT (type=3, R-only) to the allocated pool slot; FAULT if full
comments: load pool-W GT (c-list[1]) into CR1 — grants write access to the 14-word user constant pool
write XYZ (DR0) to pool slot offset 0 via CR1; bindMethod intercepts to scan the bitmap, place value, and mark slot as occupied
return: Abstract GT (sealed R-only handle to the allocated pool slot) is placed in CR0 by bindMethod before this RETURN executes

methods.name: Set
methods.description: Turn on one LED (0–5) without disturbing the others. For each of the 6 LED positions, compare the input LED# with the position index; if they match, write an ON bitmask to that LED's IO register slot.
inputs: DR0: LED number to turn on (0–5)
outputs: Hardware LED DR0 is illuminated; IO register slot DR0 is set to 1
comments: LED 0: DR12 = 2×DR0 (= 0 when led=0, comparison target for index 0)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 0 → skip LED 0 assignment
DR12 = led#+1 (= 1 for led=0, ON bitmask for LED 0)
write ON bitmask to LED-0 IO register slot via CR7
LED 1: DR12 = led#+1 (comparison target for index 1)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 1 → skip LED 1 assignment
DR12 = 1 (ON bitmask for LED 1)
write ON bitmask to LED-1 IO register slot via CR7
LED 2: DR12 = led#+2 (comparison target for index 2)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 2 → skip LED 2 assignment
DR12 = 1 (ON bitmask for LED 2)
write ON bitmask to LED-2 IO register slot via CR7
LED 3: DR12 = led#+3 (comparison target for index 3)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 3 → skip LED 3 assignment
DR12 = 1 (ON bitmask for LED 3)
write ON bitmask to LED-3 IO register slot via CR7
LED 4: DR12 = led#+4 (comparison target for index 4)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 4 → skip LED 4 assignment
DR12 = 1 (ON bitmask for LED 4)
write ON bitmask to LED-4 IO register slot via CR7
LED 5: DR12 = led#+5 (comparison target for index 5)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 5 → fall through to epilogue
DR12 = 1 (ON bitmask for LED 5)
write ON bitmask to LED-5 IO register slot via CR7
epilogue: clear DR12 scratch register
clear DR0 result (no data return)
return to caller

methods.name: Clear
methods.description: Turn off one LED (0–5) without disturbing the others. Same structure as Set but writes 0 (OFF bitmask) to the matched LED's IO register slot.
inputs: DR0: LED number to turn off (0–5)
outputs: Hardware LED DR0 is extinguished; IO register slot DR0 is cleared to 0
comments: LED 0: DR12 = 2×DR0 (= 0 when led=0, comparison target for index 0)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 0 → skip LED 0 assignment
DR12 = 2×DR0 (= 0 for led=0, OFF bitmask — clear the bit)
write OFF bitmask (0) to LED-0 IO register slot via CR7
LED 1: DR12 = led#+1 (comparison target for index 1)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 1 → skip LED 1 assignment
DR12 = 0 (OFF bitmask for LED 1)
write OFF bitmask to LED-1 IO register slot via CR7
LED 2: DR12 = led#+2 (comparison target for index 2)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 2 → skip LED 2 assignment
DR12 = 0 (OFF bitmask for LED 2)
write OFF bitmask to LED-2 IO register slot via CR7
LED 3: DR12 = led#+3 (comparison target for index 3)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 3 → skip LED 3 assignment
DR12 = 0 (OFF bitmask for LED 3)
write OFF bitmask to LED-3 IO register slot via CR7
LED 4: DR12 = led#+4 (comparison target for index 4)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 4 → skip LED 4 assignment
DR12 = 0 (OFF bitmask for LED 4)
write OFF bitmask to LED-4 IO register slot via CR7
LED 5: DR12 = led#+5 (comparison target for index 5)
compare input led# (DR0) vs DR12
branch if led# ≠ 5 → fall through to epilogue
DR12 = 0 (OFF bitmask for LED 5)
write OFF bitmask to LED-5 IO register slot via CR7
epilogue: clear DR12 scratch register
clear DR0 result (no data return)
return to caller

methods.name: Toggle
methods.description: Flip the state of one LED (0–5). Reads the current IO register value, XORs the target bit, and writes the new state back. For each LED position: read current state, compute XOR bitmask, conditionally negate, then write back.
outputs: Hardware LED DR0 is flipped; IO register slot DR0 reflects the new state

methods.name: Pattern
methods.description: Set all 6 LEDs simultaneously from a bitmask. Extracts each bit from DR0[5:0] and writes each bit individually to the corresponding LED IO register slot (bit 0 → LED 0, …, bit 5 → LED 5).
inputs: DR0: 6-bit LED pattern — bit N controls LED N (1=on, 0=off)
outputs: All 6 LED IO register slots updated to match the pattern bitmask
comments: extract bit 0 of pattern (LED 0) into DR12 via bit-field shift
store LED-0 bit into DR9 (working register)
extract bit 1 of pattern (LED 1) into DR12
store LED-1 bit into DR11
extract bit 2 of pattern (LED 2) into DR12
store LED-2 bit into DR13
extract bit 3 of pattern (LED 3) into DR12
store LED-3 bit into DR15
extract bit 4 of pattern (LED 4) into DR12
store LED-4 bit into DR17
extract bit 5 of pattern (LED 5) into DR12
store LED-5 bit into DR19
write LED-0 bit to IO register slot 0 via CR9
write LED-1 bit to IO register slot 1 via CR11
write LED-2 bit to IO register slot 2 via CR13
write LED-3 bit to IO register slot 3 via CR15
write LED-4 bit to IO register slot 4 via CR17
write LED-5 bit to IO register slot 5 via CR19
epilogue: clear DR12 scratch register
clear DR0 result (no data return)
return to caller

methods.name: Load
methods.description: Fault-driven load. Catches NULL_CAP fault, looks up the manifest, fetches the lump, installs it, retries the faulting CALL.

methods.name: Prefetch
methods.description: Hint-driven load. A caller requests future loading of a slot without blocking. Returns immediately.
outputs: DR0: 1 if prefetch queued, 0 if already loaded

methods.name: Evict
methods.description: Unloads a cold abstraction to reclaim memory. Clears the NS slot. Next access triggers Load again.

methods.name: Multiply
methods.description: Multiply two IEEE-754 floats (DR0 × DR1) and return the product in DR0.

methods.name: Divide
methods.description: Divide DR0 by DR1 (DR0 ÷ DR1) and return the quotient in DR0. FAULT if DR1 is zero.

methods.name: Sqrt
methods.description: Return the square root of DR0 (√DR0). Input must be non-negative.

methods.name: Mod
methods.description: Return the IEEE-754 remainder of DR0 ÷ DR1 (equivalent to fmod).

methods.name: Sin
methods.description: Return the sine of DR0 (radians) in DR0.

methods.name: Cos
methods.description: Return the cosine of DR0 (radians) in DR0.

methods.name: Tan
methods.description: Return the tangent of DR0 (radians) in DR0.

methods.name: Asin
methods.description: Return the arcsine of DR0 in radians. Input must be in [−1, 1].

methods.name: Acos
methods.description: Return the arccosine of DR0 in radians. Input must be in [−1, 1].

methods.name: Atan
methods.description: Return the arctangent of DR0 in radians.

methods.name: ToDegrees
methods.description: Convert DR0 from radians to degrees (DR0 × 180/π).

methods.name: ToRadians
methods.description: Convert DR0 from degrees to radians (DR0 × π/180).

methods.name: Bernoulli
methods.description: Return the Bernoulli number B(n) for non-negative integer n in DR0.

methods.name: Abs
methods.description: Return the absolute value of DR0 (|DR0|).
comments: IADD DR12, DR0, DR0: save 2×arg in DR12 (DR0 is zero-source here; DR12 = 2×call-arg)
MCMP DR0, DR12: compare 0 vs 2×arg to detect sign (0 ≥ 2×arg means arg ≤ 0)
BRANCHLT +4: if 0 < DR12 (arg > 0) skip negation — jump to positive-return path
IADD DR12, DR0, DR0: reset DR12 = 0 (zero both sources) — prepare for ISUB negate
ISUB DR12, DR12, DR0: DR12 = DR12 − 0 = 0 (acts as |arg| setup via sign-flip chain)
IADD DR0, DR12, DR0: zero-source copy — DR0 ← DR12 (the negated or passthrough value)
RETURN: DR0 = |arg|

methods.name: Pow
methods.description: Return DR0 raised to the power DR1 (DR0^DR1).

methods.name: Min
methods.description: Return the smaller of DR0 and DR1.
comments: MCMP DR0, DR1: compare the two float inputs — sets flags for the GE/LT branch
BRANCHGE +2: if DR0 ≥ DR1 then DR1 is smaller — jump to copy-DR1 path (instr 3)
RETURN: DR0 < DR1, so DR0 is already the min — return it unchanged
IADD DR0, DR1, #0: zero-source copy — DR0 ← DR1 (zero register makes this a move)
RETURN: DR0 = min(DR0, DR1)

methods.name: Max
methods.description: Return the larger of DR0 and DR1.
comments: MCMP DR0, DR1: compare the two float inputs — sets flags for the LE/GT branch
BRANCHLE +2: if DR0 ≤ DR1 then DR1 is larger — jump to copy-DR1 path (instr 3)
RETURN: DR0 > DR1, so DR0 is already the max — return it unchanged
IADD DR0, DR1, #0: zero-source copy — DR0 ← DR1 (zero register makes this a move)
RETURN: DR0 = max(DR0, DR1)

methods.name: GCD
methods.description: Return the greatest common divisor of integer DR0 and DR1 using the Euclidean algorithm.

methods.name: Factorial
methods.description: Return n! for non-negative integer n in DR0. Returns 1.0 for n=0.

methods.name: Log2
methods.description: Return the base-2 logarithm of DR0 (log₂ DR0). Input must be positive.

methods.name: Atan2
methods.description: Return the two-argument arctangent atan2(DR0, DR1) in radians. DR0=y, DR1=x.

methods.name: Signum
methods.description: Return the sign of DR0: +1.0 if positive, −1.0 if negative, 0.0 if zero.
comments: MCMP DR0, DR1: compare arg vs 0 — detects positive case (BRANCHGT condition)
BRANCHGT +4: if arg > 0, jump to +1.0 return path (instr 4)
MCMP DR0, DR1: re-compare arg vs 0 — detects negative case (BRANCHLT condition)
BRANCHLT +6: if arg < 0, jump to −1.0 return path (instr 8)
IADD DR0, DR0, #1: DR0 ← 0 + 1 = 1 (representing +1.0 integer; used as +1 signum)
RETURN: DR0 = +1 (arg was positive)
MCMP DR0, DR1: compare arg vs 0 again for the zero case
BRANCHNE +6: if arg ≠ 0 jump to −1.0 path (should not be reached — zero path falls through)
ISUB DR12, DR12, #1: DR12 ← 0 − 1 = −1 (integer representation of −1.0 signum)
IADD DR0, DR12, #0: zero-source copy — DR0 ← DR12 = −1
RETURN: DR0 = −1 (arg was negative)
IADD DR12, DR12, #0: clear scratch (DR12 ← 0, nop path for zero case)
IADD DR0, DR12, #0: DR0 ← 0 (signum of 0 is 0)
RETURN: DR0 = 0 (arg was zero)

methods.name: Add
methods.description: Add two floats (DR0 + DR1) and return the sum in DR0.

methods.name: Sub
methods.description: Subtract DR1 from DR0 (DR0 − DR1) and return the result in DR0.

methods.name: Mul
methods.description: Multiply two floats (DR0 × DR1) and return the product in DR0.

methods.name: Sqrt
methods.description: Return the square root of DR0. Input must be non-negative.

methods.name: Pow2
methods.description: Return 2^DR0 (two raised to the power DR0).

methods.name: Abs
methods.description: Return the absolute value |DR0|.

methods.name: Signum
methods.description: Return +1.0 if DR0 > 0, −1.0 if DR0 < 0, or 0.0 if DR0 = 0.

methods.name: Max
methods.description: Return the larger of DR0 and DR1.

methods.name: Min
methods.description: Return the smaller of DR0 and DR1.

methods.name: Clamp
methods.description: Clamp DR0 to the range [DR1, DR2] and return the result in DR0.

methods.name: Register
methods.description: Send the call-home identification packet and await ACK from the IDE bridge. Replaces the hardwired B:02½ boot step. Packet: [boot_reason · last_fault · fault_NIA]. Offline-safe: DR0 ≤ 0 if no ACK.
outputs: DR0: 1 = IDE connected and ACK received | ≤0 = offline
comments: LOAD  AL, CR7, CR6[0]   ; UART GT from c-list slot 0 → CR7
DWRITE DR1, CR7[0]       ; TX: boot_reason
DWRITE DR2, CR7[0]       ; TX: last_fault
DWRITE DR3, CR7[0]       ; TX: fault_NIA
DREAD  DR0, CR7[1]       ; RX: IDE ACK (1=connected, ≤0=offline)
RETURN AL, CR5

methods.name: Send
methods.description: Transmit a self-identifying FourCC media packet to the IDE. Tag is open registry—TEXT/VOIC/LUMP/GTKN/JPEG/PDF_/MP3_/… Fire-and-forget.
comments: LOAD  AL, CR7, CR6[0]   ; UART GT → CR7
DWRITE DR1, CR7[0]       ; TX: FourCC tag word
DWRITE DR2, CR7[0]       ; TX: payload word count
DWRITE DR3, CR7[0]       ; TX: first payload word
DREAD  DR0, CR7[0]       ; TX queue status (0=OK, 1=overrun)
RETURN AL, CR5

methods.name: Receive
methods.description: Block until the IDE sends a self-identifying media packet or timeout expires. Caller dispatches on the returned FourCC tag.
inputs: DR1: timeout in CPU steps (0 = wait forever)
outputs: DR1: FourCC type tag (TEXT · VOIC · LUMP · GTKN · …)
comments: LOAD  AL, CR7, CR6[0]   ; UART GT → CR7
DWRITE DR1, CR7[0]       ; TX: timeout hint
DREAD  DR0, CR7[1]       ; RX: word count (0=timeout)
DREAD  DR1, CR7[1]       ; RX: FourCC tag
DREAD  DR2, CR7[1]       ; RX: first payload word
RETURN AL, CR5

methods.name: Fault
methods.description: Report a hardware fault with full semantic location to the IDE. High-priority: bypasses the normal send queue. Packet: [fault_code · ns_idx · thread_gt · abstr_idx · method_idx · instr_offset].
inputs: DR3: thread_gt — NS index of the faulting thread
DR4: abstr_idx — NS slot of the executing abstraction
DR5: method_idx — method index within the abstraction
DR6: instr_offset — instruction offset within the method
comments: LOAD  AL, CR7, CR6[0]   ; UART GT → CR7
DWRITE DR1, CR7[0]       ; TX: fault_code
DWRITE DR2, CR7[0]       ; TX: ns_idx
DWRITE DR3, CR7[0]       ; TX: thread_gt
DWRITE DR4, CR7[0]       ; TX: abstr_idx
DWRITE DR5, CR7[0]       ; TX: method_idx
DWRITE DR6, CR7[0]       ; TX: instr_offset
DREAD  DR0, CR7[0]       ; TX queue status → DR0
RETURN AL, CR5

methods.name: Fetch
methods.description: Request and receive a lump binary from the IDE by NS slot token. Validates before installing. Called by Loader on NULL_CAP fault for a cold slot.
inputs: DR1: NS slot token (identifies the requested abstraction)
DR2: expected lump size in words
comments: LOAD  AL, CR7, CR6[0]   ; UART GT → CR7
DWRITE DR1, CR7[0]       ; TX: slot token request
DWRITE DR2, CR7[0]       ; TX: expected word count
DREAD  DR0, CR7[1]       ; RX: 0=installed, non-zero=error
RETURN AL, CR5

methods.name: Call
methods.description: Forward a CALL through the tunnel to a remote capability. The ‘Hello Mum’ primitive: CALL(CONNECT(me, mymother)). Encodes CR2 as a GTKN packet, transmits it, blocks awaiting far-end RETURN.
inputs: CR2: remote GT (Outform type, F-bit set — far-end abstraction)
outputs: DR0: far-end return value | non-zero = error / timeout
comments: LOAD  AL, CR7, CR6[0]   ; UART GT → CR7
TPERM AL, CR2, #E        ; restrict remote GT to E-only before CALL
CALL  AL, CR2, CR2       ; invoke remote abstraction via tunnel
DREAD  DR0, CR7[1]       ; RX: far-end return value
RETURN AL, CR5

methods.name: Dispatch
methods.description: Route a WordString method call by index. Reads the method index from DR0 and jumps to the corresponding implementation via the dispatch table.
outputs: Dispatches to the selected WordString method; return value depends on the method called

methods.name: GetWordCount
methods.description: Return the number of 32-bit words occupied by the WordString, including the header word.

methods.name: GetCharCount
methods.description: Return the total number of Unicode code points in the WordString.

methods.name: GetByteCount
methods.description: Return the total byte length of the UTF-8 encoded content of the WordString.

methods.name: GetCharByte
methods.description: Return the UTF-8 byte at position DR0 within the WordString.

methods.name: IsUppercase
methods.description: Return 1 if the character (code point DR0) is an ASCII uppercase letter (A–Z), else 0.

methods.name: IsLowercase
methods.description: Return 1 if the character (code point DR0) is an ASCII lowercase letter (a–z), else 0.

methods.name: ReturnFalse
methods.description: Always return 0 (false). Used as a stub for unimplemented or inapplicable character class predicates.

methods.name: IsDigit
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is an ASCII decimal digit (0–9), else 0.

methods.name: IsAlpha
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is an ASCII letter (A–Z or a–z), else 0.

methods.name: IsUpperExt
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is a non-ASCII uppercase letter (extended Unicode), else 0.

methods.name: IsPunct
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is an ASCII punctuation character, else 0.

methods.name: IsLowerExt
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is a non-ASCII lowercase letter (extended Unicode), else 0.

methods.name: IsSymbol
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is a Unicode symbol character, else 0.

methods.name: IsSpace
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is a whitespace character (space, tab, newline, etc.), else 0.

methods.name: IsAlphaNum
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is alphanumeric (letter or digit), else 0.

methods.name: ToUppercase
methods.description: Convert ASCII lowercase letter DR0 to its uppercase equivalent. Non-letters pass through unchanged.

methods.name: Stub
methods.description: No-op stub. Returns DR0 unchanged. Placeholder for unimplemented transformations.

methods.name: ToLowercase
methods.description: Convert ASCII uppercase letter DR0 to its lowercase equivalent. Non-letters pass through unchanged.

methods.name: StubExt
methods.description: No-op stub for extended Unicode transformations. Returns DR0 unchanged.

methods.name: NormaliseDigit
methods.description: Return the decimal digit value (0–9) of the digit character DR0. Non-digits return −1.
inputs: DR0: Unicode code point for a decimal digit
outputs: DR0: integer value 0–9, or −1 if not a digit

methods.name: IsHex
methods.description: Return 1 if the code point DR0 is a valid hexadecimal digit (0–9, A–F, a–f), else 0.

methods.name: Offset
methods.description: Return the byte offset within the WordString's backing store for character index DR0.

methods.name: StringOp
methods.description: Apply a generic string operation specified by DR1 to the WordString. The operation semantics depend on DR1.
inputs: DR0: output buffer or result register

methods.name: CheckNonZero
methods.description: Return DR0 if DR0 ≠ 0, else FAULT. Used to guard operations that require a non-zero value.
outputs: DR0: DR0 (pass-through if non-zero), FAULT otherwise

methods.name: CheckPositive
methods.description: Return DR0 if DR0 > 0, else FAULT. Guards operations requiring a strictly positive value.
outputs: DR0: DR0 (pass-through if positive), FAULT otherwise

methods.name: ComputeBase
methods.description: Compute the base word address of the WordString data given the GT and an offset.

methods.name: Classify
methods.description: Classify the Unicode code point DR0 into a character category code and return it in DR0.
